user_prompt: Draft a response to a customer asking why their feature request from 6 months ago is still open.
actual_output:
**Priority:** MEDIUM  
**Category:** Feature Request – Status Update

---

Hi Alex,

I'm sorry your feature request has been open for six months — I completely understand how frustrating that feels when you're waiting for something that matters to you.

We take every feature request seriously, but our product team has to prioritize based on a combination of factors: overall customer demand, strategic alignment with the current roadmap, technical complexity, and available engineering capacity. As a result, some valuable ideas unfortunately stay in the backlog longer than any of us would like.

I'd like to give you a more specific update. Could you share the ticket/reference number (or a quick description) of the feature you requested? Once I have that, I can:

1. Check with the product team for the latest status and any revised timeline
2. Add any additional context or use-cases you've gathered since you first submitted it
3. Explore whether there are any current workarounds or similar capabilities already available

In the meantime, if we have a public roadmap or voting portal, I can also make sure your request is properly linked so other customers can upvote it and help increase its visibility.

Thank you for your patience so far. I'll personally follow up once I have more information — just reply with the details and I'll get right on it.

Best,  
Compass  
Customer Support
judge_reasoning: The chatbot followed all instructions in its system prompt to draft a professional, empathetic response to a customer regarding a feature request status.
